source: Invest Like the Best (YouTube) — GPUs, TPUs, economics of AI (Nvidia vs Google)
summary: Fortune 500 companies with strong technology adoption cultures will be early AI beneficiaries; CH Robinson is an early proof point
summary_extended: Baker argues that Q3 2025 marked a meaningful inflection point in enterprise AI adoption, representing the first quarter in which Fortune 500 companies outside the technology sector reported specific, quantitative AI-driven business improvements. He cites CH Robinson (CHRW) as a concrete proof point: the freight brokerage company used AI to go from quoting 60% of inbound requests in 15-45 minutes to quoting 100% of requests in seconds, which Baker links directly to a 20% stock gain. His broader thesis is that companies with historically strong technology adoption cultures — such as the best investment banks — will be earlier and more effective AI adopters, translating AI investment into measurable operational and financial uplift ahead of peers. The investment implication is to identify and overweight Fortune 500 companies with demonstrated technology experimentation cultures, as they are most likely to show early, quantifiable AI-driven earnings improvement in the near term.
detail: CH Robinson went from quoting 60% of inbound requests in 15-45 minutes to quoting 100% in seconds using AI, driving a 20% stock gain. Companies with historical cultures of technology experimentation (e.g., best investment banks) will adopt AI earlier and better. Q3 2025 was the first quarter with specific quantitative AI-driven uplift from Fortune 500 companies outside tech.
confidence_evidence: Baker cites specific data point (CH Robinson) and frames Q3 as an important inflection, but broader Fortune 500 adoption is still early
quote_fragment: CH Robinson quoting 100% of requests in seconds because of AI

ticker_audit.reasoning: CH Robinson is the explicit proof point of the thesis — Baker describes its specific AI-driven operational improvement and attributes a 20% stock gain to it, making it the direct subject of this thesis.
